title: The US Is About to Deport an Immigrant to the Center of the Ebola Crisis
gist: A 78-year-old retired Colorado pastor facing deportation to Uganda after 20+ years of failed asylum litigation has deteriorated from active to wheelchair-bound during nine months in ICE detention, illustrating how the Trump administration's simultaneous cuts to foreign aid and surge in immigration enforcement funding are colliding at the individual level, with Uganda and DR Congo at the epicenter of an Ebola outbreak that has killed 300+ people and is worsened by the same aid cuts.

themes.theme: Policy collision: aid cuts meet enforcement surge

insights.claim: Since January 2025, the US government has drastically cut foreign assistance, effectively dismantling USAID, while simultaneously pumping billions of dollars into DHS and immigration enforcement operations.
insights.speaker: Vittoria Elliott
insights.so_what: Documents a direct fiscal reallocation from international aid to domestic enforcement under the current administration — a structural budget shift relevant to any analysis of US soft-power retrenchment and its downstream humanitarian effects.
insights.quote: Since January 2025, the US government has strangled foreign assistance, killing off the US Agency for International Development almost entirely. Meanwhile, it has pumped billions of dollars into the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) and immigration operations.

insights.claim: The Ebola outbreak in Uganda and DR Congo, which has killed more than 300 people, has been exacerbated by cuts to US foreign aid that left local organizations without funding for staff or protective equipment.
insights.speaker: Vittoria Elliott
insights.so_what: A direct causal chain from US aid-cut policy to a worsening active disease outbreak is a concrete, measurable second-order effect of the administration's foreign-aid retrenchment.
insights.quote: The outbreak has been exacerbated due to cuts to US foreign aid, leaving many local organizations without the funding necessary to employ local staff or afford protective equipment.

insights.claim: The CDC issued a travel health notice for Uganda earlier in 2026 encouraging 'enhanced precautions' due to the Ebola outbreak, yet the US government is proceeding with deportations to the same country.
insights.speaker: Vittoria Elliott
insights.so_what: A direct contradiction between the US government's own public-health guidance and its immigration-enforcement actions, illustrating siloed policy execution across agencies.
insights.quote: Earlier this month, the US Centers for Disease Control (CDC) issued a travel health notice for Uganda, encouraging travelers to practice 'enhanced precautions.'

insights.claim: Edward Nalwamba, a 78-year-old retired pastor, has been held in ICE detention at a GEO Group-run facility in Aurora, Colorado since September 2025 after his prior 'order of supervision' was revoked.
insights.speaker: Vittoria Elliott
insights.so_what: Names the specific private-prison operator (GEO Group) profiting from detention capacity amid the enforcement funding surge — relevant to tracking private-detention industry revenue drivers.

insights.claim: Nalwamba's health declined from being 'active' enough to do tasks of a 50-year-old to being confined to a wheelchair over nine months in detention, according to a pastor who visited him regularly.
insights.speaker: Vittoria Elliott
insights.so_what: A first-hand, dated before/after health comparison documents rapid physical deterioration in detention, a data point relevant to broader scrutiny of ICE detention medical-care adequacy.
insights.quote: I was just with him last Tuesday. When he was picked up, he was very active. I mean, he was doing stuff that a 50-year-old would be doing. Now he's in a wheelchair.

insights.claim: Blood work obtained in December showed abnormalities indicative of malnourishment, and Nalwamba was found to have pneumonia that detention center staff did not inform him about.
insights.speaker: Vittoria Elliott
insights.so_what: Documented medical neglect (undisclosed pneumonia diagnosis, malnutrition indicators) is a specific, evidentiary data point for any future legal or regulatory scrutiny of detention healthcare standards.
insights.quote: We finally got copies of his blood work that there were a few abnormalities that would be indicative of malnourishment, and he had pneumonia, which he didn't even know

insights.claim: Nalwamba's attorney could not obtain his complete case file because a prior attorney 'disappeared without filing the application,' and Freedom of Information Act requests for his immigration records have returned only a 'fraction of a file,' with the attorney citing this as 'a big problem with the government under the current administration.'
insights.speaker: Vittoria Elliott
insights.so_what: Suggests systemic FOIA/records-processing degradation under the current administration is directly impairing due-process access for detainees — a broader institutional-capacity signal beyond this single case.
insights.quote: This has been a big problem with the government under the current administration

insights.claim: Nalwamba claims he was detained and interrogated by Ugandan security forces in 2001 for refusing to instruct his church congregation to vote for President Yoweri Museveni, who has ruled Uganda since 1986 and continues targeting political opposition.
insights.speaker: Vittoria Elliott
insights.so_what: Provides the underlying political-persecution claim basis for the asylum case, relevant context for assessing the merits of returning him to an authoritarian regime he says specifically targeted him.

insights.claim: A DHS spokesperson said ICE follows applicable health and safety guidelines including State Department travel advisories during removal operations and that detainees receive three meals a day and access to medical care.
insights.speaker: Vittoria Elliott
insights.so_what: The government's official rebuttal/position statement, useful as the counter-claim against the attorney's and pastor's first-hand accounts of neglect.
insights.quote: ICE follows all applicable health and safety guidelines, including those outlined in the U.S. Department of State's travel advisories, when conducting removal operations
